content: read reviews/884.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/884.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/884.md.

# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist — `deepmind/884.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 884`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/884`.
- [ ] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem. **The module docstring contains only the heading, reference link, and bibliography entry. The plain-English description appears in the theorem docstring instead.** The checklist requires it in the module docstring. Minor issue — the description is present in the file, just in the wrong location.
- [x] Optional bibliography entry: `[Er98]` Erdős (1998) is cited.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos884 ... end Erdos884`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos884`).
- [x] `open Finset` appears **before** the namespace block (line 28).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- N/A: No custom `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_884` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 11]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 32–42) describing the problem, its mathematical content, and a cross-reference to problem #144.
- [x] Theorem docstring references source paper `[Er98]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks "Is it true that …" — a yes/no question, so **Case 1** applies.

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (line 45).
- [x] The RHS (`P`) formalizes the question as originally asked: there exists an absolute constant `C > 0` such that for all `n ≥ 1`, the double sum `∑_{i<j} 1/(d_j - d_i)` over all pairs of divisors of `n` is at most `C * (1 + ∑ 1/(d_{i+1} - d_i))` over consecutive divisor pairs. This correctly captures the `≪` (implied constant) formulation.
- [x] `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` would mean "no" to the original question.
- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)` (inside the RHS of `↔`), not as theorem parameters.
- [x] Consecutive divisor condition is correctly formalized: `p.1 < p.2 ∧ ∀ e ∈ n.divisors, ¬(p.1 < e ∧ e < p.2)` — no divisor of `n` lies strictly between `p.1` and `p.2`.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research open`.
- [x] `research open` is a valid category value.
- [x] Theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body (consistent with `research open`).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 11`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (one: 11).
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order (only one number; trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] 11 (number theory) is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` format (multi-line, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations (none present).
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. The problem is a single question. The docstring cross-references problem #144, but no variant theorems are defined.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout (4-space continuation for theorem type signature is standard Lean style).
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `ℝ`, `ℕ`, `∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `≥`, `≤`, `¬`, `∧`, `↔`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run during this review. No obvious issues; expected `sorry` warning only.
- [ ] Custom linters not run during this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (all substantive items pass; one minor note)

The file is clean, well-structured, and the mathematical formalization is correct. One non-blocking note:

1. **Module docstring plain-English description** (§3): The module docstring lacks a brief description of the problem — it has only the heading, reference link, and bibliography. The plain-English description is present in the theorem docstring (lines 32–42) but the checklist asks for it in the module docstring. Consider moving or duplicating a one-sentence summary into the `/-! ... -/` block.
2. **Build verification** (§12): Not performed during this review; should be confirmed in CI.

    20→# Erdős Problem 884
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/884](https://www.erdosproblems.com/884)
    23→
    24→[Er98] Erdős, P., _Some of my new and almost new problems and results in
    25→combinatorial number theory_ (1998).
    26→-/
    27→
    28→open Finset
    29→
    30→namespace Erdos884
    31→
    32→/--
    33→Is it true that, for any $n$, if $d_1 < \cdots < d_t$ are the divisors of $n$, then
    34→$$\sum_{1 \leq i < j \leq t} \frac{1}{d_j - d_i} \ll 1 + \sum_{1 \leq i < t} \frac{1}{d_{i+1} - d_i},$$
    35→where the implied constant is absolute? [Er98]
    36→
    37→The double sum over all pairs of divisors is bounded (up to an absolute constant)
    38→by $1$ plus the sum over consecutive divisor gaps. Two divisors are consecutive
    39→if no other divisor of $n$ lies strictly between them.
    40→

    42→-/
    43→@[category research open, AMS 11]
    44→theorem erdos_884 :
    45→    answer(sorry) ↔
    46→    ∃ C : ℝ, C > 0 ∧ ∀ n : ℕ, n ≥ 1 →
    47→      (∑ p ∈ (n.divisors ×ˢ n.divisors).filter (fun p => p.1 < p.2),
    48→        (1 : ℝ) / ((p.2 : ℝ) - (p.1 : ℝ))) ≤
    49→      C * (1 + ∑ p ∈ (n.divisors ×ˢ n.divisors).filter (fun p =>
    50→        p.1 < p.2 ∧ ∀ e ∈ n.divisors, ¬(p.1 < e ∧ e < p.2)),
    51→        (1 : ℝ) / ((p.2 : ℝ) - (p.1 : ℝ))) := by
    52→  sorry
    53→

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 884

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/884](https://www.erdosproblems.com/884)

[Er98] Erdős, P., _Some of my new and almost new problems and results in
combinatorial number theory_ (1998).
-/

open Finset

namespace Erdos884

/--
Is it true that, for any $n$, if $d_1 < \cdots < d_t$ are the divisors of $n$, then
$$\sum_{1 \leq i < j \leq t} \frac{1}{d_j - d_i} \ll 1 + \sum_{1 \leq i < t} \frac{1}{d_{i+1} - d_i},$$
where the implied constant is absolute? [Er98]

The double sum over all pairs of divisors is bounded (up to an absolute constant)
by $1$ plus the sum over consecutive divisor gaps. Two divisors are consecutive
if no other divisor of $n$ lies strictly between them.

See also problem #144.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_884 :
    answer(sorry) ↔
    ∃ C : ℝ, C > 0 ∧ ∀ n : ℕ, n ≥ 1 →
      (∑ p ∈ (n.divisors ×ˢ n.divisors).filter (fun p => p.1 < p.2),
        (1 : ℝ) / ((p.2 : ℝ) - (p.1 : ℝ))) ≤
      C * (1 + ∑ p ∈ (n.divisors ×ˢ n.divisors).filter (fun p =>
        p.1 < p.2 ∧ ∀ e ∈ n.divisors, ¬(p.1 < e ∧ e < p.2)),
        (1 : ℝ) / ((p.2 : ℝ) - (p.1 : ℝ))) := by
  sorry

end Erdos884

content.text: The review **passed**. The summary says "**Result: PASS**" with only non-blocking notes (module docstring description placement and build verification not run). No changes needed.
